data.title: The 'Stock Picker's Market' Is Back — But The S&P 500 Is Still Hiding It
data.description:
If you only glance at the S&P 500's flat year-to-date performance, you might think 2026 has thus far been dull. But that’s not so.

Beneath the surface, something important is happening: stocks are no longer moving in sync.

The era of buying the index and riding mega-cap tech higher is giving way to a far more selective environment — one where individual names, sectors and narratives matter again.

According to Adam Turnquist, LPL's chief technical strategist, that’s “the return of the stock picker's market.”

AI Trade: From Euphoria To Disruption

For most of 2025, the market rewarded anything tied to artificial intelligence. Investors chased productivity gains, capital spending cycles and explosive earnings growth. AI exposure meant premium valuations.

But that narrative shifted last fall.

Instead of simply asking “Who wins from AI?", investors began asking "Who gets disrupted by it?"

“The perceived threat of traditional business models being displaced by accelerating AI capabilities has created a growing list of winners and losers well beyond the software sector,” Turnquist said.

The Great Rotation Out Of Big Tech

Another major force at work: capital rotation.

Technology stocks – as tracked by the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSE:XLE) – make up roughly one-third of the S&P 500.

So when investors trim exposure to mega-cap growth and reallocate into value stocks, small caps or international equities, the ripple effects are amplified.

Turnquist describes it as a "drinking water from a fire hose" effect — and it's visible in performance dispersion across previously overlooked corners of the market.

The S&P 493 — which excludes the Magnificent Seven — is projected to grow 2026 earnings by nearly 14%, up from roughly 10% last year.

That's a meaningful broadening of earnings growth beyond mega-cap leadership.

Sector leadership has also flipped as many of 2025's underperformers are now leading, while several of last year's darlings lag.

While industrials remain strong, the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSE:XLE) — just 3.2% of the S&P 500 — has surged more than 20% year to date, fueled by record ETF inflows.

Active Management's Comeback

Flows confirm the shift. Passive investing still dominates total assets, but active equity ETFs now account for roughly 32% of ETF flows, up dramatically from just 6% in 2021.

That change matters.

When correlations are high, stock picking is difficult — everything moves together. But when correlations fall and dispersion rises, differentiation creates alpha potential.

The CBOE S&P 500 Dispersion Index is near multi-month highs. Meanwhile, the CBOE Three-Month Implied Correlation Index is relatively low — meaning the market expects more idiosyncratic moves, not broad synchronized rallies.

In simple terms: stock stories matter again.

What It Means For Investors

On the surface, the market looks calm. Underneath, it's rotating, fragmenting and repricing.

Shifts in the AI narrative. Rotation away from mega-cap concentration. An improving macro backdrop supported by Fed rate cuts and easing inflation. Accelerating flows into active vehicles.

Together, these forces are creating wider performance gaps across sectors and stocks.

For investors, the implication is clear: broad beta exposure may no longer be enough.

Image created using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin Trade Sideways In A Tuesday Muted Session
data.description:
Bitcoin is holding around $68,000 level, reflecting broader market softness and persistent fear sentiment.

Notable Statistics:

  • Coinglass data shows 83,760 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours for $196.52 million.
  • SoSoValue data shows net inflows of $15.2 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Friday. Spot Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $10.3 million.
  • In the past 24 hours, top losers include pippin, MYX Finance and Quant.

Notable Developments:

Trader Notes: Position trader Bob Loukas said Bitcoin's price action remains notably weak. Even after the recent flush lower, the market has not produced the kind of sharp countertrend bounce typically seen in oversold conditions.

According to Loukas, Bulls need to reclaim $72,000 to begin discussing a meaningful relief rally and until then, price behaviour resembles classic bear market structure

Crypto trader Jelle highlighted historical cycle durations.

Bitcoin bull markets to last around 150 weeks and bear markets take around a year to fully play out.

If the current cycle follows similar timing, he suggests the bear phase may not fully bottom until around October.

Analyst Titan of Crypto noted that Bitcoin has entered its production cost zone, with the lower boundary around $44,500. Historically, including in 2014 and 2022, Bitcoin briefly dipped below this band before forming a structural bottom and beginning a broader recovery.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: We're Close to Retirement With $900,000 in Home Equity — Should We Open a HELOC First?
data.description:
A couple preparing to retire within the next year recently shared their situation on Reddit, outlining a financial profile that would look strong by most measures. 

Both partners are 52, their home is paid off and valued around $900,000. They hold more than 33 times their annual expenses in investments and roughly two and a half years of expenses in cash.

Despite that cushion, they are considering opening a home equity line of credit now, before leaving the workforce. Their thinking is centered on access. Products like a Rocket Mortgage HELOC can be easier to qualify for while income is still coming in, and the couple views the line as a backstop rather than a source of ongoing cash flow.

The concern they raised is sequence-of-returns risk, which refers to the impact of poor market performance in the early years of retirement, when withdrawals begin before portfolios have time to grow or recover. Selling investments during a downturn can permanently reduce a portfolio's ability to support future spending, even if markets rebound later.

For retirees drawing from savings while markets fall, more shares must be sold to cover the same expenses. That accelerates depletion and limits the benefit of later recoveries. Even moderate declines early in retirement can shorten portfolio longevity.

That is where a HELOC can fit into a broader retirement plan. With a line of credit already in place, a retiree can temporarily draw against home equity during a market downturn instead of selling investments at lower prices. Once markets recover, withdrawals can shift back to the portfolio and the balance can be repaid.

Opening a HELOC before retirement allows homeowners to qualify based on current income and debt levels. After retirement, income often drops to Social Security and portfolio withdrawals, which may not meet lender requirements for a large line.

The couple on Reddit said that they would not plan to use the line unless a prolonged downturn depleted their cash reserves. In that context, the HELOC functions as an insurance layer rather than leverage. It provides flexibility without requiring large cash balances that could drag on long-term returns.

Alternatives exist, including holding more cash, reducing withdrawals during downturns, or working longer. 

In the Reddit couple's case, the strategy aligns with their broader financial position. Their assets are sufficient, their debt is minimal, and their goal is protection rather than consumption. Used carefully, a HELOC can support that goal by reducing pressure on investment portfolios during early retirement.

The approach is a contingency tool. When opened early and used selectively, a HELOC can help retirees manage timing risk without forcing permanent changes to their investment strategy.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Newmont Stock Dips As Lunar New Year Reduces Demand
data.description:
Newmont Corp (NYSE:NEM) shares are under pressure Tuesday as Lunar New Year market closures across Asia coincide with investor caution ahead of Wednesday's Federal Reserve meeting minutes. Here's what you should know today.

  • Newmont stock is facing resistance. Why is NEM stock retreating?

Pressure On Precious Metals

The weakness follows an unusually volatile stretch for precious metals. Gold recently surged past $5,595 an ounce before collapsing toward $4,400 in just two days at the end of January.

Focus Turns To Fed Minutes

Traders are also keeping a cautious stance ahead of Wednesday's release of the Fed's January meeting minutes. Reuters reports that investors are looking for more insight into how policymakers are thinking as the central bank approaches a leadership handoff.

President Donald Trump has nominated former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh to succeed Chair Jerome Powell when his term ends in May. Markets don't anticipate a rate change until the June 16–17 meeting.

Fed officials, including Powell, have said they expect inflation to ease back toward 2% by mid‑year, though several policymakers — including Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee — want to see clearer evidence before acting.

The Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the PCE index, has hovered around 2.8% since May. December PCE data arrives Friday, and officials have signaled they aren't expecting much improvement.

Mixed Technical Setup

Newmont is currently facing some bearish pressure as it trades below key moving averages, indicating a potential weakness in its price action. Traders should be cautious as the stock has recently broken below support, which could signal further downside.

The RSI is currently neutral, suggesting that there isn’t any strong momentum pushing the stock either way at this moment. However, the recent entry into overbought territory on Jan. 12 could indicate that the stock may have reached a short-term peak.

MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure and suggesting that the momentum may not be in favor of buyers right now. Traders should look for signs of a reversal or further confirmation of a downtrend before making any moves.

Key support is at $108.00, while resistance is at $135.00. If Newmont Corporation’s stock tests the support level, a break below could lead to a more significant decline, while a move above resistance could signal a potential recovery.

The golden cross in April, when the 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA, indicated a bullish trend at that time.

Over the past 12 months, Newmont’s stock has seen a remarkable increase of 159.29%, reflecting a strong longer-term trend. This performance highlights the stock’s ability to recover from its 52-week low of $41.23, but the current positioning near the high of $134.88 raises questions about sustainability.

Currently, Newmont’s stock is positioned at 87.4% of its 52-week range, indicating that it is trading near its highs. This positioning suggests that while the stock has performed well, it may be vulnerable to pullbacks if it cannot maintain momentum.

NEM Price Action: Newmont shares were down 3.10% at $121.90 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: AeroVironment Stock Soars As JPMorgan Sets $320 Target
data.description:
AeroVironment Inc (NASDAQ:AVAV) shares are jumping higher Tuesday after JPMorgan kicked off coverage with an Overweight rating and a $320 price target.

  • AeroVironment shares are powering higher. Why is AVAV stock up today?

What's Driving The Surge

JPMorgan analyst Seth Seifman said AeroVironment is positioned to deliver mid‑teens growth thanks to its exposure to some of the fastest‑growing areas in defense, including drones, counter‑drone systems and space, CNBC reported. He noted that the company's experience in these segments puts it in a strong spot as demand accelerates both in the U.S. and abroad.

Seifman also pointed out that AeroVironment stands to benefit as the Department of War looks to expand the industrial base and adopt a more commercial approach to procurement.

Premium Valuation Justified

Seifman argued that AeroVironment's tailwinds support a higher valuation. He noted that the stock has historically traded at an average of 4.7x forward sales since 2020, but said a premium is warranted given rising U.S. and international defense budgets, heightened geopolitical risk and the defense department’s push to expand the industrial base.

AeroVironment shares are up 55% over the past year, though they've been mostly flat so far this year.

What Does The Technical Picture Show?

AeroVironment is currently positioned in a challenging technical setup, having recently broken below key support levels. This indicates potential bearish momentum, which traders should watch closely as it could signal a shift in trend direction.

The RSI is currently neutral, suggesting that there isn’t strong momentum pushing the stock in either direction. However, it previously entered overbought territory on Jan. 8, indicating that the stock may have experienced excessive buying pressure at that time.

MACD is currently below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure in the short term, suggesting that traders should be cautious, as it reflects a weakening momentum that could lead to further downside.

Key support is at $240.00, while resistance is at $293.00. If AeroVironment’s stock tests the support level and holds, it may indicate a potential reversal, but a break below could lead to further declines.

Over the past 12 months, AeroVironment’s stock has gained 70.37%, reflecting a strong upward trend despite recent volatility.

AVAV Price Action: AeroVironment shares were up 7.36% at $261.81 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Piotr Swat/Shutterstock
